title: Opposition and Sweep
setup: Your kings stand in **opposition** on the e-file, and the rook waits on the 7th rank for the kill.
hint: Lift the rook to the 8th rank — your king on **e6** holds {{d7}}, {{e7}} and {{f7}}.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is the bedrock **K+R vs K mate** position taught in every book. The kings face each other in opposition; the rook delivers from afar.

Play {{Ra8#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{d8}} and {{f8}} — covered by the rook on a8 (8th rank)
- {{d7}}, {{e7}}, {{f7}} — all controlled by your king on **e6**
- The rook on a8 is too far away for {{Kxa8}}
- Nothing can interpose on the 8th rank

> **Takeaway:** Memorize this exact pattern: kings in opposition + rook on the same rank = mate. It's the destination of nearly every K+R vs K endgame.
